finding: Canonical-only queue reading loses valid later owner-scoped records; effective state must be derived from immutable canonical history plus accepted additive layers.

observed: operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json contains M-001 but not the later completed M-002 or M-003 records.
M-003 explicitly hands off creation of a machine-readable effective-state resolver.
The specialist registry already contains the required continuity roles.
inferred: A resolver can preserve history while presenting a current effective view if it never mutates canonical input and rejects cross-owner updates.
proposed: Use tools/continuity/effective-queue-state.mjs as the read-time composition layer for canonical and additive queue records.
superseded: Reading ACTIVE_QUEUE.json alone as current project state is superseded by layered resolution once additive owner records exist.
unresolved: The committed Node test suite was not executed in this connector-only run.
External deployment, test, and peer-run claims still require direct evidence beyond structural validation.

verification.source_behavior: GitHub accepted the module and deterministic test artifacts as additive commits.
verification.falsification_route: Run node --test tools/continuity/effective-queue-state.test.mjs; then supply malformed, cross-owner, and unsupported-claim records and require explicit rejection without canonical mutation.
